mnemonics: C H A D S W A P (for Disk Scheduling Algorithms): C-SCAN, C-LOOK, FCFS, SSTF, SCAN, C-SCAN, C-LOOK
keyPoints: Disk scheduling algorithms (e.g., SSTF, SCAN, C-SCAN) optimize seek time and disk bandwidth.
Formatting creates a file system structure with boot blocks, volume control blocks, and directory structures.
RAID levels (0-6) provide different combinations of performance, redundancy, and capacity through disk striping and mirroring.
commonMistakes: Confusing the direction and behavior of SCAN (elevator) and C-SCAN (circular elevator) algorithms.
Assuming a higher RAID level is always better, without considering the specific trade-offs in performance, cost, and redundancy.
Believing that bad-block handling is entirely automatic and requires no manual intervention or dedicated hardware support.
